title: Dryopteris lacera
intro: Dryopteris lacera, also known as male fern, is a species of fern native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds and distinctive male and female reproductive structures. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to alleviate digestive issues, reduce menstrual cramps, and soothe respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris lacera include lignans, terpenes, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Dryopteris lacera is often combined with other plants, such as lady's mantle and yarrow, to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that are used to treat a range of health issues, including anxiety, indigestion, and menstrual disorders.
taxonomy: Dryopteris lacera is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which is a group of vascular plants commonly known as the wood ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a part of the larger group of ferns known as the polypod ferns. The polypod ferns are classified under the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. The kingdom to which Dryopteris lacera belongs is Plantae, encompassing all flowering plants, conifers, and other vascular plants.
